_comment: Pinned OSDU PDM v1.0 subset used by the generator (ADR 0010). Column names are verbatim from the OSDU PDM Data Dictionary (Apache-2.0). Each table entry: canonical OSDU table name, the exact columns (name + coarse type) this generator populates, and the enumerated reference-data values it emits. Both src/oag_generator/schema.py and tests/test_conformance.py are checked against this file, so emitted Parquet cannot silently drift in name, type, or reference value.
_reference_value_casing: OSDU reference-data values are seed data whose casing is not standardized across R_ tables (the dictionary shows e.g. 'Forecast', 'DAY', 'measured', 'OIL'). We deliberately normalize the values we emit to Title Case and pin them here as the authoritative set.
_types: int -> pa.int64, string -> pa.string, float -> pa.float64

well._note: FACILITY_ID is a denormalized FK to FACILITY (the well's battery), added for the asset-rollups hierarchy Well -> Facility -> Field (#8, ADR 0021). PPDM links a well to a facility via a junction; we carry the flat FK as a deliberate simplification.

down_time_event._note: EVENT_CATEGORY carries the downtime cause (the R_EVENT_CATEGORY value column); it is user-extensible reference data in OSDU, so its values are NOT pinned here. DURATION_HOURS is per-value hours (an implicit UOM, per ADR 0017). Nullable audit/subcategory/id columns from the dictionary are omitted (ADR 0017).

well_test._note: OSDU PDM WELL_TEST (verified present in the OSDU PDM v1.0 Well-Test data model, 2026-07; the PPDM-3.9 WELL_TEST family is lineage only, ADR 0010); keyed to the WELL directly (WELL_ID/UWI). Each rate value carries its own per-value OUOM column (the OSDU PDM stores units per measured value). Deliberate simplifications (ADR 0019): the OSDU model splits rates across child tables WELL_TEST_FLOW_PERIOD / WELL_TEST_FLOW_MEASUREMENT / WELL_TEST_MEASUREMENT -- we denormalize the oil/gas/water test rates onto WELL_TEST; TEST_TYPE is the flat R_WELL_TEST_TYPE value column (carried like REPORTING_ENTITY_KIND); a test spans a single TEST_DATE; validation/choke/pressure, run-number and audit columns are omitted. WKS analogue: work-product-component--FlowTest.

rpen_allocation_factor._note: OSDU PDM RPEN_ALLOCATION_FACTOR -- the OSDU-published production allocation-factor table, which records each 'from' RPEN (reporting entity) and 'to' RPEN and the allocation factor (verified against the OSDU PDM v1.0 Volume-Relevant + Well-Test data-model pages, 2026-07). RPEN = REPORTING_ENTITY, so this is natively a from-entity -> to-entity factor; the PPDM-3.9 analogue is PDEN_ALLOC_FACTOR (lineage only, ADR 0010). NOT a stored allocated-volume table (PDEN_VOL_ALLOC is out of scope). Deliberate simplifications (ADR 0019): we carry flat FROM_/TO_REPORTING_ENTITY_ID keys (from = Field-kind group entity, to = Well-kind entity); ALLOCATION_FACTOR carries its own per-value OUOM ('fraction', dimensionless); qualifier/method and audit columns are omitted. Exact OSDU column spellings were not machine-verified from the deep dictionary page, so the column profile below is a deliberate ADR-0019 selection.

facility._note: OSDU PDM FACILITY (PPDM-3.9-based) -- the surface-facility master. Its primary key is the pair (FACILITY_ID, FACILITY_TYPE): a battery is a FACILITY_TYPE *value* (R_FACILITY_TYPE), not its own table. Links to FIELD (FIELD_ID) and OPERATOR; carries a centroid latitude/longitude, each with its own per-value OUOM column ('dega', decimal degrees), mirroring the OSDU per-value OUOM pattern. Deliberate simplifications (ADR 0021): the well->facility link is a flat FACILITY_ID FK on WELL (PPDM uses a junction); operator/field are flat columns; the FACILITY family's status/class/audit and CRS columns are omitted; a facility carries a single centroid, not a footprint. WKS analogue: master-data--GenericFacility. Exact column spellings were not machine-verified from the deep dictionary page, so the columns below are a deliberate ADR-0021 profile selection.
